title: Leucanthemopsis cuneata
intro: Leucanthemopsis cuneata, commonly known as Alpine Daisy or Alpine White Daisy, is a perennial alpine plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemopsis cuneata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations.
